questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt:
With reference to "Digital Twin Technology", consider the following statements:
1. It is restricted to the digital representation of inanimate physical objects and cannot model human beings or abstract processes.
2. It relies on continuous real-time data from sensors to simulate behavior and monitor operations.
3. It enables organizations to simulate real-world scenarios and predict outcomes before implementing physical changes.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: A digital twin is a digital representation of a physical object, person, or process. It is not restricted to inanimate objects. Statement 2 is correct: It uses real-time data from sensors for behavior simulation and operation monitoring. Statement 3 is correct: It helps organizations simulate real situations and their outcomes, ultimately allowing them to make better decisions.
questions.takeaway: Digital twins are dynamic, real-time updated models that can represent objects, people, or processes to simulate outcomes and monitor operations.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: A digital twin is a digital representation of a physical object, person, or process, contextualized in a digital version of its environment. It can help an organization simulate real situations and their outcomes... It uses real-time data from sensors for behavior simulation and operation monitoring.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the core mechanism that distinguishes a "Digital Twin" from a conventional static 3D model or historical simulation?

options.text: It operates exclusively in a virtual reality environment without any data connection to the physical world.
It uses real-time data from sensors attached to a physical counterpart to continuously update its simulation and monitor operations.
It relies entirely on historical data to predict the future lifecycle of a product without requiring active monitoring.
It is a purely theoretical framework used to design physical objects before they are manufactured.

questions.explanation: A digital twin is contextualized in a digital version of its environment and uses real-time data from sensors for behavior simulation and operation monitoring. This continuous flow of real-time data makes it dynamic and sets it apart from static models or purely historical simulations.
questions.takeaway: The defining feature of a digital twin is its continuous integration of real-time sensor data from its physical counterpart to simulate behavior and monitor operations.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It uses real-time data from sensors for behavior simulation and operation monitoring.
